skill.name: azure-mgmt-weightsandbiases-dotnet
skill.description: Guidance for using the Azure Resource Manager Weights & Biases SDK for .NET to create, inspect, update, and delete Azure Marketplace W&B instances.
skill.summary: Manage Azure Marketplace Weights & Biases instances with the .NET Azure Resource Manager SDK.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick, URL, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples and public reference links in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, malicious network behavior, or weak cryptographic implementation were found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. SKILL.md contains fenced documentation examples for dotnet package installation and SDK usage, with no script file, automation hook, or user-controlled command execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation and fenced code snippets. There is no executable wrapper, shell script, or instruction that automatically runs these commands.

low_findings.title: Public Reference URLs and Example Service URL
low_findings.description: The network findings are low-risk documentation references. The URLs point to NuGet, W&B documentation, Azure Marketplace, Azure GitHub source, and an example W&B host used by SDK users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited URLs are visible reference links or an example login host. No hidden request, credential forwarding, or data exfiltration code is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak cryptography alerts are false positives at the cited lines, which contain the skill description and an Azure resource lookup example. The reconnaissance alert is an Azure exception message example, not local system discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not implement cryptography or enumerate the host environment. They are descriptive text and Azure SDK error-handling examples.

content.value_statement: ML platform teams need repeatable guidance for Azure-hosted Weights & Biases resources. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce .NET ARM SDK workflows for provisioning, SSO, updates, deletion, and reference checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to install the Azure.ResourceManager.WeightsAndBiases and Azure.Identity packages.
Shows how to authenticate with DefaultAzureCredential and an ArmClient.
Guides creation of Azure Marketplace Weights & Biases instances with offer and user details.
Documents read, list, update, delete, and name availability workflows for W&B resources.
Summarizes SSO configuration, supported regions, core SDK types, and related Azure SDK packages.
limitations: The skill is documentation only and does not execute Azure operations by itself.
The SDK version shown is a preview release, so APIs may change.
Users must supply valid Azure subscriptions, permissions, marketplace terms, and W&B settings.
The W&B Python SDK section is brief and does not cover full experiment tracking design.

use_cases.title: Provision W&B for an ML Platform
use_cases.description: Create a repeatable .NET workflow for deploying a Weights & Biases instance through Azure Marketplace.

use_cases.title: Review Enterprise Access Setup
use_cases.description: Plan Entra ID SSO settings and domain configuration before enabling enterprise access for W&B users.

use_cases.title: Maintain Existing W&B Resources
use_cases.description: List resources, inspect provisioning state, apply tags, and remove obsolete W&B instances.

prompt_templates.title: Create a First Instance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline the steps for creating an Azure Weights & Biases instance with the .NET ARM SDK. Include package setup, authentication, marketplace details, and required user settings.

prompt_templates.title: List and Inspect Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to explain how I can list all Azure Weights & Biases instances in a resource group and subscription. Include the fields I should inspect for health and location.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Enterprise SSO
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft a .NET approach for enabling SAML SSO on an Azure Weights & Biases instance. Include Enterprise App ID, allowed domains, and update flow considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Lifecycle Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a full lifecycle process for Azure Weights & Biases resources in .NET. Cover create, validate, tag, list, update, delete, error handling, and preview SDK risks.

output_examples.input: Create an Azure W&B instance for an ML platform team.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step plan covering package installation, Azure authentication, marketplace offer settings, admin user details, regional placement, managed identity, and provisioning-state checks.

output_examples.input: Help me add enterprise SSO to an existing W&B deployment.
output_examples.output: A focused implementation outline that retrieves the instance, updates SAML SSO settings, includes allowed domains, and saves the changed resource through the collection update flow.

output_examples.input: Prepare operations guidance for existing W&B resources.
output_examples.output: A checklist for listing instances, reviewing region and provisioning state, applying tags, handling Azure errors, and deleting retired instances after confirmation.

best_practices: Confirm Azure Marketplace terms and subscription permissions before creating W&B resources.
Use DefaultAzureCredential and managed identity where possible for safer cloud authentication.
Check provisioning state and add tags for ownership, environment, and cost tracking.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode real subscription IDs, admin emails, tenant data, or API keys in prompts or source files.
Do not delete W&B instances without confirming retention needs for experiments and models.
Do not assume preview SDK APIs are stable across package upgrades.

faq.question: Does this skill create Azure resources automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users must run reviewed code with their own Azure credentials.

faq.question: Which package does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Azure.ResourceManager.WeightsAndBiases for .NET and uses Azure.Identity for authentication.

faq.question: Can it help with Entra ID SSO?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes SAML SSO properties, allowed domains, and the update flow for an existing instance.

faq.question: Is the SDK version stable?
faq.answer: The skill references a preview package and preview API version. Users should verify current SDK behavior before production use.

faq.question: Does it replace the W&B experiment tracking SDK?
faq.answer: No. It manages the Azure resource. Experiment tracking still uses the W&B SDK after deployment.

faq.question: What permissions are needed?
faq.answer: Users need Azure permissions to manage the target subscription, resource group, marketplace purchase, and identity settings.
